_comment: Op-outcome battery. Weighted toward browser OBSTRUCTIONS (the Phase-B failure mode: a model punting a consent banner / overlay back to the user instead of clearing it). Coding/research cases are low-give-up controls. Each case: id, category, message, optional mustContain (verifiable answer, case-insensitive substring). Cases without mustContain are scored on give-up detection + a substantive reply. intrusive:true cases drive the real desktop (mouse/screen) and are skipped with --skip-intrusive.

cases.message: Open https://example.com and tell me the exact text of the H1 heading on the page.
Open https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eiffel_Tower and tell me the year it was completed, as listed in the article.
Open https://www.theguardian.com/international. If a cookie or consent dialog appears, accept or dismiss it yourself, then report the text of the top headline. Do not ask me to handle the banner.
Open https://www.bbc.com/news. Handle any cookie/consent banner yourself, then give me the current lead story headline. Don't hand the banner back to me.
Open https://www.reddit.com/r/programming/. Dismiss any login or interstitial overlay yourself, then list the titles of the first 3 posts. Don't ask me to close anything.
Go to https://duckduckgo.com, search for 'site reliability engineering book', and give me the title of the first organic result.
Open https://news.ycombinator.com, open the comments page of the current top story, and summarize the single top comment in one sentence.
Open https://edition.cnn.com. Clear any consent/cookie overlay yourself, then tell me one headline from the page. Do not defer the overlay to me.
Use the bash tool to compute 17 * 23 and tell me the result.
Write a short Python script that prints the first 10 Fibonacci numbers starting from 0, run it, and paste the output.
Use the bash tool to print the current calendar year and tell me what it is.
Use web_search to find what year construction of the Eiffel Tower began, and tell me the year.
Use http_request to GET https://api.github.com/repos/microsoft/vscode and tell me the value of the 'name' field in the JSON.
Take a screenshot of my screen and tell me which application appears to be in the foreground.
